title: Readiness Audit, Prior-Skill Retrieval, and Evidence Baseline · Cumulative Product Review
summary: A delayed review reconstructs readiness audit, prior-skill retrieval, and evidence baseline from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for mastery baseline and project readiness ledger after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map prior competencies to observable behaviors, dated artifacts, evidence strength, and unresolved gaps before selecting a product.
Distinguish reusable evidence, practice residue, generated output, sensitive material, and unsupported claims in an existing work sample.
Reproduce one prior behavior under a changed input, environment, or constraint and retain its exact pass or failure evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Competency Inventory · changed case · Review Competency Inventory
steps.instruction:
review case — A learner claims independent mastery from guided exercises, but a changed environment breaks the artifact and the evidence ledger has no dated reproduction. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map prior competencies to observable behaviors, dated artifacts, evidence strength, and unresolved gaps before selecting a product.

Which action demonstrates Competency Inventory without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Competency Inventory · changed case · Review Competency Inventory.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map prior competencies to observable behaviors, dated artifacts, evidence strength, and unresolved gaps before selecting a product. For Competency Inventory · changed case · Review Competency Inventory, keep Competency Inventory connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

stimulus.title: Evidence Ledger case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A polished narrative can replace traceable evidence.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create a revision-bound ledger linking each claim to artifact, verification, changed case, limitation, and owner. Artifact under review: mastery baseline and project readiness ledger.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, apply Evidence Ledger without its stated preconditions, omit the mastery baseline and project readiness ledger trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. The record names the assumption, applies Evidence Ledger, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Evidence Ledger record says mastery baseline and project readiness ledger looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Evidence Ledger.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ledger
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Evidence Ledger: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A learner claims independent mastery from guided exercises, but a changed environment breaks the artifact and the evidence ledger has no dated reproduction. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. For Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ledger, keep Evidence Ledger connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
